name: Chevin Eco
classification_notes: A display variant of the Chevin sans-serif with decorative knocked-out circular holes producing a neon-light effect; the cutouts are the defining feature rather than any structural typographic classification.
summary: A display variant of Chevin with knocked-out circular counters that mimic a neon-light effect and reduce ink consumption when printed.
description: Originally produced for a Royal Mail trade magazine, Chevin Eco punches circular holes through the letterforms of the parent Chevin typeface to create a glitzy, neon-adjacent aesthetic. The toner-saving properties only take effect in print, making the eco credential largely incidental to the decorative appeal. Two weights, Bold and ExtraBold, cover West European Latin with standard OpenType features including fractions, ligatures, ordinals and superiors.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Chevin Eco as a whimsical display font, a variation on the original Chevin with knocked-out circular holes that produce a glitzy neon effect while saving toner in print. Developed originally for a Royal Mail trade magazine, they call it simultaneously glamorous, green and good fun, if a tad gimmicky. The family comprises two weights: Chevin Eco Bold and Chevin Eco ExtraBold.

name: Accent Graphic
classification_notes: Accent Graphic is described as a sans typeface with the thick/thin contrast of a serif, with flared stems — placing it in the tradition of flared or stressed sans typefaces, distinct from pure geometric or grotesque forms.
summary: A flared sans with serif-like thick/thin contrast, originally drawn in 1997 as a corporate typeface for a London design consultancy.
description: Designed by Nick Cooke and originating from a client commission, Accent Graphic applies the optical contrast of a serif to a sans skeleton, with flared stems lending it a delicate, refined quality. The family runs to three weights — Light, Medium and Bold — in OpenType format with coverage for Western, Central and Eastern European languages. Its understated elegance has seen it used by luxury brands, including Salvatore Ferragamo for their Signorina fragrance line.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Accent Graphic as one of their earliest releases, originally developed as the corporate font for a London design consultancy in 1997, starting from the word 'accent' in lower case. They position it as essentially a sans typeface with the thick/thin contrast of a serif, noting that its flared stems and graceful strokes give it broad appeal and a fit for branding and luxury applications.

name: Amulet
classification_notes: Amulet is an uncial display typeface with Celtic calligraphic roots, drawing on the Gaelic manuscript tradition rather than any conventional serif or sans-serif structure.
summary: A Celtic uncial display face inspired by ancient Irish manuscripts and the Book of Kells, intended for headlines, posters and film titles.
description: Drawn after a visit to Dublin and a study of manuscript traditions at Trinity College, Amulet takes the uncial letterform as its basis and inflects it with Gaelic calligraphic character. The single Medium weight suits large-scale headline use where its scribal, monastic quality can read clearly. The foundry notes an almost Arabic flavour in certain letterforms, giving the face an ambiguity that extends its range beyond strictly Celtic contexts.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Amulet as a distinctive headline font with Gaelic roots, ideal for posters, film titles and any design calling for a scribal, monastic look. They trace its Celtic calligraphic influence to an encounter with ancient manuscripts and the Book of Kells in Dublin, and note the font carries an air of spiritual mysticism alongside an almost Arabic quality in some of its forms.

name: Copperscript
classification_notes: A constructed Copperplate Script derived from English Roundhand, placing it firmly in the formal script tradition with deliberate structural modernity applied over the archaic style.
summary: A constructed Copperplate Script by Nick Cooke, derived from English Roundhand and updated with a modern weight axis and contextual lead-in and end characters.
description: Built on the tradition of English Roundhand, the design runs across six weights from ExtraLight to ExtraBold, with a variable font bundled in the family pack for infinite intermediate steps. Contextual alternates trigger lead-in and end characters automatically, and three stylistic sets offer alternate capitals, plus variants for the lowercase 'r' and 'z'. Directional arrows and an extended Latin character set with broad European language coverage round out a practical formal script for display and packaging work.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Copperscript as a constructed Copperplate Script that applies a modern typographic twist to an archaic style. Designed by Nick Cooke, it sits at the crossroads where elegance meets constructed letterforms, offering six base weights, a variable version, contextual lead-in and end characters, directional arrows, alternate caps and stylistic set variations for select lowercase letters.

name: Chevin Pro
classification_notes: Chevin is a rounded, slightly condensed sans-serif with open counters and restrained letterforms, placing it firmly in the humanist sans tradition with a functional, legibility-first approach.
summary: A rounded, slightly condensed humanist sans in six weights, best known as Royal Mail's corporate typeface since 2007.
description: Designed with open counters and a restrained construction, Chevin Pro is particularly well suited to signage and corporate identity work where legibility at small sizes matters. Erik Spiekermann has noted its balance of charm without cuteness. The Pro version extends beyond the standard Latin to cover Greek and Cyrillic scripts, with OpenType features including small caps, stylistic alternates and tabular figures.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Chevin Pro as a modern, rounded type family in six weights designed with functionality and legibility in mind. Its slightly condensed proportions and open counters make it suitable for both text and signage use. The Pro variant adds Greek and Cyrillic script support, extended OpenType features and broad language coverage across West European, Central and East European, Baltic, Turkish, Cyrillic and Greek writing systems. Chevin has been Royal Mail's corporate font since 2007 and appears across every Post Office in the UK.

name: Chevin Std
classification_notes: Chevin Std is a rounded, slightly condensed humanist sans-serif with open counters and restrained letterforms designed for legibility at text and signage sizes. Its functional construction and warm proportions place it firmly in the humanist sans tradition.
summary: A rounded humanist sans in six weights, designed for legibility at small sizes and signage, best known as Royal Mail's corporate typeface since 2007.
description: Chevin Std is the stripped-back variant of Chevin Pro, covering West European, Central and East European, Baltic and Turkish Latin scripts without the Greek, Cyrillic or small caps features of its larger sibling. Its open counters and slightly condensed proportions make it practical for both continuous text and wayfinding environments. Erik Spiekermann has noted its legibility in small sizes, attributing it to the typeface's restrained shapes and simple construction.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Chevin Std as a modern, rounded type family in six weights built for functionality and legibility, with open counters and a slightly condensed style suited to both text and signage. It is the reduced-feature version of Chevin Pro for users who do not require Greek and Cyrillic scripts, multiple numeral sets or small caps. The family has been Royal Mail's corporate typeface since 2007, appearing across every Post Office in the UK.

name: Digitalis
classification_notes: A single-weight display typeface built on minimal strokes with rounded terminals and high thick-thin contrast, placing it firmly in the display category with a geometric, futuristic character.
summary: A single-weight rounded display face by Nick Cooke, built on a stripped-back stroke economy that creates an unexpected legibility despite strong thick-thin contrast.
description: Designed with the aim of reducing each character to its most essential strokes, Digitalis achieves a pared-back geometry that reads as simultaneously futuristic and approachable. The high contrast between thick and thin strokes gives it a distinctive rhythm that holds up at headline sizes for posters, banners and magazine covers. It ships as a single OpenType OTF file with extended Latin coverage spanning West European, Central and East European, Baltic and Turkish languages.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Digitalis as an original, aesthetically pleasing rounded typeface built from a minimum of strokes, with each character reduced to only its most essential elements. They note that the contrast between thick and thin strokes makes it surprisingly legible as text, and that despite its ultra-modern, almost futuristic appearance it retains a friendly, rounded quality suited to posters, banners, web pages and magazine headlines.

name: Gatterberg
classification_notes: Gatterberg is explicitly classified as a hybrid typeface combining slab serif uppercase letters with sans serif lowercase, a combination unusual enough to place it in the display category rather than either slab or sans-serif alone.
summary: A hybrid typeface drawn from a nineteenth-century Salzburg crypt inscription, pairing slab serif capitals with a sans serif lowercase across four weights.
description: Nick Cooke derived Gatterberg from lettering on a family crypt in Salzburg, where he found slab serif capitals sitting alongside a sans serif lowercase, a combination he initially misremembered as a conventional serif. The four upright styles run from Thin to Bold with no italic, and the family includes small caps, alternates for G, J and g, and two stylistic sets. A variable font is bundled with the family pack, and language coverage spans West European, Central and East European, Baltic and Turkish scripts.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Gatterberg as a hybrid typeface mixing slab serif caps with a sans serif lowercase, inspired by lettering on a family crypt in Salzburg. The character set includes small caps and alternates for G, J and g, with extended Latin language coverage across West European, Central and East European, Baltic and Turkish languages. The family comes in four upright styles from Thin to Bold, with a variable font included free in the family pack.

name: Exentrica
classification_notes: Exentrica draws directly from Viennese Secession typography of 1898–1903, placing it firmly in the art nouveau display tradition with its decorative stylistic alternates and contrast between monoline and high-contrast stroke variants.
summary: A display family by Nick Cooke rooted in Viennese Secession typography, offered in Monoline and Contrast subfamilies across four weights with extensive OpenType variation.
description: Drawn from Nick Cooke's encounter with Secession-era work at Vienna's Museum of Applied Arts, Exentrica distils the movement's typographic character into a family that runs from Thin to Medium in both Monoline and Contrast cuts. Seven stylistic sets, contextual alternates and discretionary ligatures give designers a wide platform for typographic experimentation, with all style sets designed to work together in combination. A variable version is included free with the full family purchase.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Exentrica as the product of Nick Cooke's visit to the Museum of Applied Arts in Vienna, where the work of the Secession artists made a strong impression. The family merges various Secessionist design strands into a single type family with numerous stylistic variations, available in both Monoline and Contrast versions across weights from Thin to Medium, with a free variable version bundled with the family volume.

name: Geetype
classification_notes: A single-weight decorative display face with art deco characteristics: buxom, voluptuous curves and a hand-lettered poster quality inspired by Cassandre's cigarette pack lettering from the 1920s–30s.
summary: A single-weight art deco display face rooted in Cassandre's 1920s cigarette pack lettering, with rounded, voluptuous curves suited to vintage poster work.
description: Drawn from a piece of lettering attributed to A.M. Cassandre, Geetype brings a hand-crafted, golden-era quality to display settings. Its buxom forms evoke the graphic sensibility of 1920s and 30s poster campaigns, with a free-flowing rhythm that works well at large sizes. Extended Latin coverage spans West European, Central and East European, and Baltic and Turkish character sets, with OpenType features including ligatures, fractions, ordinals and superiors.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Geetype as inspired by cigarette pack lettering by A.M. Cassandre, evoking the mood of the 1920s and 30s. They position it as an unusual, eye-catching single-weight display font with art deco glamour, suited to vintage poster campaigns and Hollywood golden-era aesthetics. Available in OpenType format with extended Latin language support.

name: Gizmo
classification_notes: Gizmo is a brush script drawn directly from brush pen writing on porous paper, giving it the hallmarks of that category: fluid strokes, contextual alternates and ligatures to simulate natural handwriting variation.
summary: A brush pen script drawn on porous paper and traced with precision, using contextual alternates and ligatures to simulate the rhythm of quickly-written handwriting.
description: Written with a brush pen on porous paper, scanned and traced to preserve the characteristically blobby, uneven stroke edges, Gizmo uses clever OpenType programming to vary letterforms automatically as you type. Multiple ligatures and contextual alternates prevent the repetition that tends to expose digital scripts, making it convincing at text sizes and more expressive at display scale. Language coverage spans West European, Central and East European, Baltic and Turkish Latin character sets.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Gizmo as a rustic brush script created by writing with a brush pen on porous paper, selecting the best characters, then scanning and tracing with precision to maintain maximum character integrity. Clever programming ensures an even flow when set as text, while multiple ligatures that automatically activate as you type give it a truly authentic brush script appearance and hand-drawn dynamic. It works especially well at large sizes, where the blobby and uneven edges become most evident.

name: Goskar
classification_notes: A handwritten script typeface based directly on the designer's own broad-nib fountain pen handwriting, distinguished by a reversed stress resulting from holding the pen at an unconventional angle.
summary: A broad-nib handwritten script in four weights by Nick Cooke, notable for its reversed stress and extensive OpenType programming that produces authentic character variation in running text.
description: Based on Nick Cooke's own handwriting with a fountain pen held at an unconventional angle, the reversed stress gives Goskar an unusual energy distinct from most calligraphic scripts. Four weights from Light to Black are available as standard OpenType fonts, with a single-axis variable font bundled free with the family pack for intermediate weight control. The character set is unusually generous, including small caps, arrows, emoticons, underlines, fractions and a broad range of contextual alternates and ligatures that sustain the impression of genuine handwritten flow.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Goskar as their most ambitious script font yet, named after a rock on Tenby's north shore. Nick Cooke based it on his own handwriting using a broad nib pen held at the wrong angle to produce a reversed stress. The family includes four weights plus a bundled variable font, and carries extensive OpenType features including contextual alternates, multiple ligature sets, small caps, emoticons and arrows, covering West European, Central and East European, Baltic and Turkish Latin character sets.

name: Hand Script Volume
classification_notes: A bundle of three handwriting script fonts each simulating a different writing instrument: nibbed pen, brush pen and roller pen. All three fall clearly within the script/handwriting category.
summary: A bundle of three handwriting fonts from G-Type, each simulating a distinct writing instrument: roller pen, nibbed pen and brush pen.
description: Hand Script Volume brings together Rollerscript Smooth, Olicana Fine and Gizmo Regular into a single value pack, covering three categories of handwritten feel. Olicana Fine, drawn with a nibbed pen, offers the richest feature set with ligatures, alternates, swashes, ink splats and crossings-out. Rollerscript takes a more casual, informal tone suited to roller-pen handwriting, while Gizmo replicates brush pen writing on porous paper with programming that maintains an even flow across continuous text.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Hand Script Volume as a value bundle featuring three selected styles from their most popular handwriting fonts. Three pen types are represented: nibbed pen through Olicana Fine, brush pen through Gizmo, and roller pen through Rollerscript Smooth, each delivering a distinctly different handwritten character.

name: Houschka Alt Pro
classification_notes: A geometric sans-serif with rounded letterforms inspired by 1930s signage; the Alt Pro variant swaps rounded A and W glyphs for straight alternatives, giving it a more formal, businesslike character than the standard Houschka Pro.
summary: A variant of Houschka Pro that swaps its rounded A and W for straight-sided alternates, lending the family a more formal, corporate tone across six weights and true italics.
description: Where Houschka Pro leads with rounded signature glyphs, the Alt Pro version places straight A and W forms at the default positions, making it the more restrained of the two families without altering any other aspect of the design. The 12-style family spans Thin to ExtraBold with true italics, and each font carries an extensive OpenType feature set including small caps, three stylistic sets, contextual alternates, ligatures and four numeral styles. Language coverage stretches across extended Latin and Cyrillic, supporting over 50 languages from Afrikaans to Ukrainian.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Houschka Alt Pro as a carbon copy of Houschka Pro with one key difference: the rounded signature A and W glyphs swap places with their straight alternates on the default positions, giving the family a more businesslike demeanour. The name derives from a now-defunct confectioner's shop in Salzburg with a 1930s frontage and distinctively rounded letterforms on its signage.

name: Houschka Pro
classification_notes: Monolinear structure with rounded corners and humanist proportions places it squarely in the humanist sans tradition, citing Gill Sans and Johnston as reference points while adding distinctive rounded terminals.
summary: A monolinear humanist sans with rounded corners and signature glyph eccentricities, drawing on the tradition of Gill Sans and Johnston.
description: Six weights from Thin to ExtraBold, each with a true italic, give the family 12 styles in total, all carrying extended Latin and Cyrillic coverage. Distinctive glyphs at A, G, Q, W and w give Houschka Pro a light personality that sets it apart from more neutral corporate humanists, though stylistic alternate sets allow these to be swapped for straighter forms. A companion family, Houschka Alt Pro, ships with the conventional alternates as defaults for users who prefer a cleaner corporate register.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Houschka Pro as a clean, legible modern sans with humanist qualities akin to Gill Sans and Johnston, but with a charming character of its own. Named after a Salzburg confectioner's shop with a 1930s frontage and rounded letterforms, the family offers small caps, three stylistic alternate sets, contextual alternates, ligatures and four sets of numerals across 12 fonts with extended Latin and Cyrillic support.

name: Houschka Rounded
classification_notes: A rounded geometric sans-serif with softened terminals and curves, positioned as a warmer companion to Houschka Pro, placing it firmly in the rounded geometric sans tradition.
summary: A rounded companion to Houschka Pro with softened terminals and broad language coverage across Latin and Cyrillic scripts, available in 12 styles.
description: Drawing from the same geometric foundations as Houschka Pro, this family adds rounded terminals throughout for a softer, more approachable character without sacrificing typographic weight and contrast. Twelve styles cover a wide range of weights with italics, and OpenType features include small caps, four sets of numerals, stylistic sets and ligatures. Language support extends to Central and Eastern European, Baltic, Turkish and Cyrillic scripts, making it a practical choice for international campaigns. The family notably served as the centrepiece of Visa's 'Life Flows Better' advertising campaign at the London Olympic Games.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Houschka Rounded as a companion family to Houschka Pro featuring even softer curves and terminals, while retaining full typographic impact. They suggest it as a contemporary alternative to VAG Rounded, and note its use at the London Olympic Games in Visa's advertising campaign. The family ships in OpenType OTF format with WOFF, WOFF2 and EOT webfont counterparts.

name: Houschka Rounded Alt
classification_notes: A soft-cornered linear sans with rounded terminals, placing it firmly in the geometric sans tradition; the rounded stroke endings distinguish it from a strict grotesque or humanist classification.
summary: An alternate-character variant of Houschka Rounded, swapping arched A and W for straight versions whilst retaining the full rounded geometric personality of the original.
description: Houschka Rounded Alt is identical to the parent Houschka Rounded family in every respect except that straight-sided A and W characters are set as the default, with the arched originals remaining accessible via OpenType alternates. Inspired by the rounded lettering above an old Austrian sweet shop in Salzburg once owned by Georg Houschka, the family carries 12 styles with extended Latin and Cyrillic coverage across over 50 languages. The OpenType feature set is generous, including three stylistic sets, small caps, four sets of numerals, ligatures and fractions — used commercially by brands such as Arla and the National Trust.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Houschka Rounded Alt as identical to the original Houschka Rounded family but with straight A and W characters as the default, making it the more restrained option for those who want the rounded softness without the quirky arched forms. Named after a former Austrian sweet shop in Salzburg, the font was one of G-Type's very first retail releases and comes packed with stylistic sets, ligatures, alternates and four sets of numerals across 12 styles.

name: Jekyll & Hyde
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a neo-humanist sans in its default form (Jekyll), with a grotesque variant accessible via Style Set 1 (Hyde), placing it firmly in the humanist sans tradition with grotesque alternates.
summary: A dual-personality sans from Nick Cooke that shifts between a neo-humanist default and a grotesque alternate set via OpenType stylistic sets.
description: Jekyll & Hyde packages two distinct sans-serif personalities into one family: the default Jekyll cut is neo-humanist in character, while switching to Style Set 1 reveals Hyde, a more conventional grotesque. The family spans three widths and multiple weights, with variable font files bundled into the family volumes, offering two axes for weight and width. An extended Latin character set covers West, Central and East European languages along with Baltics and Turkish, with small caps, alternates and multiple stylistic sets included.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Jekyll & Hyde as two typefaces in one, with the default humanist appearance (Jekyll) dramatically altered by Style Set 1 (Hyde), which takes on a grotesque character. Three widths and multiple weights are offered, with variable fonts included free with family purchases. Designer Nick Cooke notes a personal connection to the name, having been born in Hyde, near Manchester.

name: Morpeth
classification_notes: A condensed sans serif originally designed for a wayfinding system, with open counters and a generous x-height optimised for signage and space-constrained text settings.
summary: A condensed sans originally drawn for Morpeth Borough's wayfinding system, built for signage and space-constrained typography.
description: Designed for a municipal wayfinding brief, it carries open counters and a generous x-height that sustain legibility at small sizes and at distance. Six weights are available as OpenType fonts with small caps, discretionary ligatures, four sets of numerals and broad Latin coverage including Central European, Baltic and Turkish. Short descenders and a tight width make it a practical choice for sub-headings and body copy where space is tight.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Morpeth as a hard-working condensed sans serif originally created for Morpeth Borough's wayfinding system, making it well suited to signage. Open counters and a generous x-height give it strong legibility, and the six-weight OpenType family comes with discretionary ligatures, small caps, four sets of numerals and support for Central European, Baltic and Turkish languages.

name: Olicana
classification_notes: Olicana is a handwriting-style script mimicking fountain pen writing, with contextual ligatures and alternates that give it the irregular character of genuine handwritten text.
summary: A fountain-pen handwriting script in three variants, using OpenType contextual ligatures to reduce glyph repetition and improve authenticity.
description: Three subfamilies cover a spectrum from crude to refined: Rough for raw, unpolished results; Smooth for a more schooled feel; and Fine for a delicate touch. The font relies heavily on OpenType's contextual alternate and ligature features to swap letter combinations automatically as you type, avoiding the telltale repeated-glyph look common in lesser handwriting fonts. The character set also includes swashes, small caps, ink splats, crossing-outs, fractions and ordinals, with language support across West European, Central and East European, Baltic and Turkish Latin scripts.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Olicana as an informal script font that resembles handwriting with a fountain pen, making full use of OpenType programming to replace letter combinations with ligatures automatically. The family includes Olicana Rough, Olicana Smooth and Olicana Fine, and the fonts are packed with ligatures, alternates, swashes and extras such as ink splats and crossings-out, with the option to switch between modern and ornate styling within the same font.

name: Nubian
classification_notes: Nubian is described as a crisply modern sans serif with open, semi-extended proportions and even colour; its legibility-focused construction and text-friendly character place it in the humanist sans tradition.
summary: One of G-Type's earliest typefaces, a modern sans serif with open, semi-extended proportions designed for consistent legibility across print and screen.
description: Five weights from Thin to Bold, each with true matching italics, give the family a useful range for both text and display work. The letterforms are drawn with a noticeably open aperture and semi-extended width that produces even colour at running text sizes. Each font includes lining and old-style figures, standard and discretionary ligatures, fractions and ordinals, with coverage across West European, Central and East European, Baltic and Turkish Latin scripts.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Nubian as one of the first typefaces they ever designed: an elegantly proportioned, crisply modern sans serif family of five weights with true italics. They note its open, semi-extended appearance gives very even colour and excellent legibility as text, and that its contemporary letterforms work well at all sizes in print and on screen.

name: Precious Sans Two
classification_notes: A contemporary sans-serif with humanist tendencies, featuring quirky alternate forms retained as stylistic sets and a refined balance between personality and utility across six weights.
summary: A thorough reworking of Nick Cooke's original Precious Sans, with nearly every character redrawn and the family extended with small caps, discretionary ligatures and directional arrows across six weights.
description: G-Type's Nick Cooke rebuilt Precious Sans from the ground up, redrawing and reproporting almost every glyph rather than issuing a minor update. The quirky originals, including the extended lowercase 'f' and binocular 'g', survive as stylistic alternates via OpenType style sets, preserving the character of the first release without forcing it on users. Twelve styles cover Thin to Black with italics, and the feature set runs to small caps, proportional and tabular figures, fractions, superiors and 20 directional arrows per weight.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Precious Sans Two as a distinctively modern typeface built for advanced typographic use across print, web and digital publishing. They position it as a complete overhaul rather than a re-release, noting that nearly every character has been redrawn, re-proportioned, respaced and revamped, with the original quirkier glyphs retained as optional OpenType alternates.

name: Organon Serif
classification_notes: Organon Serif blends slab and calligraphic traits, giving it a contemporary character that sits between a traditional serif and a slab; the page describes it explicitly as a modern serif face with chunky, sturdy qualities.
summary: A six-weight modern serif blending slab and calligraphic traits, designed to pair with its companion Organon Sans.
description: Organon Serif shares metrics and stem widths with Organon Sans, allowing the two families to work together as a coordinated typographic system. The serif carries a chunky, characterful quality drawn from both slab and calligraphic influences, supported by a thorough set of OpenType features including small caps, swash alternates, old style figures and discretionary ligatures. Language coverage extends across West European, Central and East European, Baltic and Turkish scripts.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Organon Serif as a stylish, feature-laden six-weight OpenType family that complements its sister Organon Sans. The two families share similar metrics and stem widths to form an elegant and legible suite, with Organon Serif mixing slab and calligraphic traits and including professional OpenType features such as small caps, swash alternates and an extended Latin character set covering European languages, the Baltic states and Turkish.

name: Organon Sans
classification_notes: Flared, tapered stems give Organon Sans a structure closer to humanist tradition than geometric or grotesque models, with letterforms that draw on calligraphic origins while remaining clean and upright.
summary: A characterful sans with tapered, flared stems and a generous glyph set, designed as one half of the Organon type system alongside its serif companion.
description: Organon Sans pairs with Organon Serif, sharing similar metrics and stem widths to create a cohesive typographic suite. Six weights run from Light to UltraBlack, each with small caps and a full suite of OpenType features including three style sets, lining and old style numerals in proportional and tabular cuts, and standard and discretionary ligatures. Latin coverage spans West European, Central and East European, Baltic and Turkish languages.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Organon Sans as one of the hidden gems in their library, designed to perform well alongside sister typeface Organon Serif, both sharing similar characteristics and metrics. The foundry notes its flared stems give the fonts an attractively robust appearance, while generous spacing contributes to excellent legibility.

name: Precious Serif
classification_notes: A chunky, contemporary slab serif with consistent stroke widths and sturdy serifs, designed as a companion to Precious Sans and sharing its metrics and overall construction approach.
summary: A chunky contemporary slab serif by Nick Cooke, designed to pair directly with Precious Sans across print and digital work.
description: Designed by Nick Cooke, Precious Serif shares its metrics and construction with the Precious Sans family, making it well suited to mixed typesetting within a single layout. The family spans six weights from Book to UltraBlack, each with a true italic, giving twelve styles in total. Extended Latin coverage includes West European, Central and East European, Baltic and Turkish scripts, with tabular lining figures and standard OpenType features.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Precious Serif as a distinctive, modern slab serif that adds a stylish punch to any project. They position it as the sister typeface to Precious Sans, with both families sharing similar metrics and characteristics to ensure they work together in print and digital applications. The complete family offers 12 versatile fonts across 6 weights with true italics.

name: Remora Corp
classification_notes: Described explicitly as a humanist sans serif; Remora Corp is the conservative, corporate-facing variant of the Remora family, with restrained letterforms suited to business typography.
summary: The restrained, corporate-facing member of the Remora family, offered across five width sets each containing 14 styles.
description: Remora Corp pairs conservative letterforms with a comprehensive OpenType feature set including small caps, stylistic alternates and five style sets that allow selective borrowing of personality from its sibling, Remora Sans. Five width variants run from a condensed W1 to an extra-wide W5, giving each a full 14-style range spanning weights and italics. Latin coverage extends to West European, Central and East European, Baltic and Turkish languages.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Remora Corp as the straight-laced workhorse of the two Remora designs, better suited to business and corporate typography than Remora Sans. The quirks of the Sans letterforms are replaced by more conservative options with no design compromise, and users can reintroduce personality via stylistic alternates or one of five style sets available in each weight.

name: Remora Sans
classification_notes: Explicitly described as a humanist sans serif with characteristic details such as curved leg forms, spurless lowercase, and expressive italic alternates, placing it firmly in the humanist sans tradition.
summary: A wide-ranging humanist sans serif in five widths, distinguished by rounded stroke details, curved leg forms and characterful italics.
description: Seventy fonts span five widths from the condensed W1 to the extra-wide W5, each width offering 14 styles. Distinctive cap forms include a splayed M and rounded A, W and Y strokes, while the lowercase carries curved legs on J, K and R, a spurless u and an expressive italic set with alternate x, y and z. All fonts include small caps, stylistic alternates across five style sets, and both proportional and tabular lining figures.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Remora Sans as the more expressive and exuberant of the two Remora design variations, its corporate counterpart being Remora Corp. The family spans five width sets from W1 condensed to extra-wide W5, with each width containing 14 styles brimming with stylistic alternates, small caps, multiple figure sets and a full range of OpenType typographic features. Humanist letterforms have been designed throughout for optimum legibility.

name: Saltaire
classification_notes: Saltaire is explicitly a Didone typeface, characterised by extreme thick-to-thin stroke contrast in the tradition of Didot and Bodoni, with tapered stems and super-contrasted Poster styles reinforcing that classification.
summary: A Didone serif by Nick Cooke with deliberately 'lumpy' tapered stems and swash characters, designed to bring more personality to a genre that can feel predictable.
description: Nick Cooke designed Saltaire to retain the extreme thick-to-thin contrast of the Didone tradition while adding character through slightly irregular, tapered stems across a range of fourteen styles from Light to Black. Super-contrasted Poster and Poster Italic cuts extend the family for headlines and fashion editorial where maximum impact is needed. Each style ships with small caps, stylistic alternates, swash glyphs and multiple figure options via OpenType features.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Saltaire as a Didone face that was several years in the making, with designer Nick Cooke wanting to bring more personality to a genre he felt could be predictable. He introduced a slightly 'lumpy' feel with tapered stems and a Swash feature for extra typographic flair, alongside super-contrasted Poster styles intended to make a bold statement in fashion magazines and similar applications.

name: Sherborne
classification_notes: The foundry explicitly describes Sherborne as a Humanist Old Style serif, citing calligraphic roots, tapered stems, angled axes and bracketed serifs — characteristics that define the humanist serif tradition.
summary: A humanist old style serif with optically tuned subfamilies, notable for its 'waisted' vertical stems that curve inward for added elegance.
description: Sherborne draws on calligraphic tradition through tapered stems, angled axes and bracketed serifs, with a modern refinement introduced via concave vertical stems said to have been inspired by a letter from Eric Gill to Stanley Morison. Three subfamilies — Display, Headline and Text — each contain five weights from Thin to Bold with italics, giving 30 fonts in total. The subfamilies are differentiated by x-height, serif length and contrast, with Display offering the finest proportions and Text the most practical weight for reading sizes.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Sherborne as a Humanist Old Style with a modern twist, inspired by a comment from Eric Gill to Stanley Morison. Its 'waisted' vertical stems curve inward for elegance and legibility. The three family sets — Display, Headline and Text — each offer 10 styles across 5 weights plus italics, with progressively larger x-heights from Display through to Text. Extended Latin character support is included alongside OpenType features such as small caps, old-style figures, fractions and ligatures.

name: Rollerscript
classification_notes: Rollerscript is a casual handwriting script drawn with a roller pen, producing informal, connected letterforms with pronounced pressure points and authentic join behaviour, placing it firmly in the casual script category.
summary: A roller-pen handwriting script available in Smooth and Rough variants, built around over 100 automated ligatures for a convincing hand-drawn result.
description: Each character was drafted by hand with a roller pen before scanning and digitisation, giving the letterforms a more casual and informal quality than G-Type's nibbed-pen script Olicana. Pronounced pressure points at stroke starts, ends and joins contribute to its authenticity, reinforced by over 100 standard ligatures that automatically substitute common pairings like 'tt' and 'gg'. The family includes multiple underlines, sketched icons, arrows and emoticons, and covers an extended Latin character set across West European, Central and East European, Baltic and Turkish languages.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Rollerscript as a more modern, casual counterpart to their Olicana typeface, noting that the change of instrument from nibbed pen to roller pen produces a decidedly more informal outcome. They highlight over 100 automated ligatures, multiple underlines and a set of sketched icons and emoticons as key features, and position it as ideal for adding a human touch to messages, designs or brand identities.

name: Sovereign Display
classification_notes: A decorative all-caps display typeface with thin horizontal crossrules and a solid shadow creating a three-dimensional engraved appearance; best classified as an ornate display slab given its formal, certificate-ready character.
summary: An all-caps decorative display typeface with thin horizontal crossrules and a solid drop shadow that produces a formal, engraved three-dimensional effect.
description: A single-style headline font built on an engraved slab serif structure, where thin horizontal crossrules sit atop a heavier shadow to produce a pronounced three-dimensional quality. Lower case positions carry small caps, and proportional non-lining figures and ordinals are included via OpenType features. Suited to certificates, manuscripts and formal titling where an elaborate, slightly Victorian gravitas is wanted.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Sovereign Display as ornate and iconic, a decorative all-caps headline font in one style with small caps on the lower case positions. Thin horizontal crossrules underpinned by a heavier solid shadow give it a prominent three-dimensional appearance and an air of formal distinction, making it ideal for certificates, manuscripts, titling or anything requiring a sombre, elaborate or engraved slab serif style.

name: Victory
classification_notes: Designed with reference to historical ship lettering, Victory exhibits classical serif proportions with high-contrast display variants alongside text-optimised weights, placing it firmly in the classical or traditional serif category.
summary: A classical serif by Nick Cooke drawn from the lettering on the stern of HMS Victory, offered in both text and high-contrast display variants across ten styles each.
description: Inspired by the carved stern lettering of the 104-gun warship HMS Victory, Nick Cooke's design pairs standard text weights with higher-contrast display cuts, running from ExtraLight to ExtraBold in each subfamily. The complete volume bundles variable font files for both upright and italic styles, with axes for weight and optical size in a single file. Each style includes small caps, multiple figure sets and an extended character set with alternates and ligatures covering West European, Central and Eastern European, Baltic and Turkish scripts.
foundry_blurb: G-Type describe Victory as a classical serif family inspired by the lettering on the stern of HMS Victory, the world's oldest naval vessel still in commission. The family ships in standard and higher-contrast display versions across ten styles each, with variable fonts included free in the complete volume. An extensive character set covers small caps, alternates, ligatures and multiple figure sets.
